ORDER RE DEMURRER (DEFENDANT ACCEPTANCE INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ACCEPTANCE INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE INSURANCE CONTRACT INCLUDES A STANDARD "USE" CLAUSE AND THE COURT IS PRESENTED WITH A PURE QUESTION OF LAW. THE PREDOMINATING CAUSE/SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR TEST IS FAVORED. (SEE STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE CO. V. GRISHAM (2004) 122 CAL. APP. 4TH 563). APPLYING THIS TEST, THE ACCIDENT DID NOT ARISE OUT OF THE OWNERSHIP, MAINTENANCE OR USE OF AN AUTO. THE USE OF VEHICLE DID NOT CONTRIBUTE TO THE INJURY. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT THE DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
MINI-MINUTES FOR NOV-30-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-25-2011 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-03-2011 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Mar-01-11 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT ACCEPTANCE INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Guy O. Kornblum, Esq. & Dan G. Berris, Esq. of Guy Kornblum & Associates 440-7800 for pltf & opposing party (seeking clarification); Samuel M. Zaif, Esq. of Bassi Edlin Huie & Blum LLP 403-4427 for deft & moving party.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: Sustained without leave to amend. The insurance contract includes a standard "use" clause and the court is presented with a pure question of law. The predominating cause/substantial factor test is favored. (See State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Co. v. Grisham (2004) 122 Cal. App. 4th 563). Applying this test, the accident did not arise out of the ownership, maintenance or use of an auto. The use of vehicle did not contribute to the injury. No reasonable probability that the defect can be cured by amendment. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2010 Continued To Nov-30-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
